title: polyalthia spathulata
intro: Polyalthia spathulata, also known as the Indian arrowroot or polyalthia, is a plant species native to the tropical forests of Asia, characterized by its small, white flowers and edible tubers. The medicinal properties of Polyalthia spathulata include its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of consuming the plant's tubers include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and providing relief from various respiratory issues. The tubers can be prepared for medicinal use through herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and powders, which are often used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments.
